syscalls.comment: LAYER 1A — Neutralize Dirty COW race vector. Blocks the page table discard primitive (MADV_DONTNEED).
LAYER 1B — Neutralize Dirty Pipe (CVE-2022-0847). Blocks manipulation of pipe buffers linked to read-only files.
LAYER 1C — Neutralize Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431). Blocks creation of AF_ALG (38) userspace crypto sockets.

LAYER 2 — Block process injection. Prevents CAP_SYS_PTRACE abuse for cross-process memory injection (Stage 2 of the Hexa Force threat model). Required for debuggers (gdb, strace) — not needed in production containers.

LAYER 3 — Prevent filesystem namespace manipulation. Blocks attackers from remounting filesystems as read-write or creating bind mounts to access host paths. Required only for nested container engines.
LAYER 4 — Block namespace creation. Prevents creation of new user/mount/PID namespaces from within the container, closing the CVE-2022-0492 cgroup escape vector.
LAYER 5 — Block kernel keyring manipulation. Prevents abuse of the kernel key management facility for privilege escalation (CVE-2016-0728).
LAYER 6 — Block kernel replacement. Prevents loading a new kernel image from within a container.
LAYER 7 — Block kernel module operations. Prevents loading or unloading kernel modules, which could introduce rootkits.
LAYER 8 — Block process accounting manipulation. Prevents enabling BSD-style process accounting to host filesystem paths.
